primary.note: Locked: both the 1987 low and 2020 high are essentially undisputed historical fact (see module docstring for independently confirming sources, plus our own data's real corroborating price action around each), not subject to promotion. Labeled 'major secular low/high' rather than a specific wave-position number ('(IV)'/'(V)') since neither anchor's PRECEDING wave structure can be independently resolved from data that starts after it -- same restraint ^IXIC's own bootstrap applies for the identical reason (its own module docstring).

labels.granularity_note: VUSTX close-only row: h/l are the scaled close repeated into every field, not a true intraday extreme -- see TLT_LONG.json's own history_splice disclosure.
labels.date_discrepancy_note: this source's actual extreme in the search window falls on 1987-10-16, not the commonly-cited 1987-10-19 -- reported as found, not silently corrected to the textbook date

labels.granularity_note: real TLT intraday high (TLT_LONG.json's ohlc-kind rows)

own_anchor_substitutions.granularity_note: real TLT intraday high (TLT_LONG.json's ohlc-kind rows)

invalidation.basis: A confirmed close below the real 1987 low would invalidate the 'single Primary-degree advance 1987->2020' framing this bootstrap locks in.

note: TLT has no independent bootstrap; it mechanically inherits TLT_LONG's primary-degree anchor (1987, 2020) per dashboard_build_spec.md §2/§8, regenerated every build run and never hand-edited. 1 anchor label(s) fall within TLT's own history (starts 2002-07-30) and are substituted in own_anchor_substitutions below.
